bead.title: agent: cleanly unwind interrupted work and try attempts
bead.description:
PROBLEM
After the root context is cancelled by Ctrl-C/SIGTERM, `ddx work` and `ddx try` must finish the TD-031 interruption path: preserve available evidence, classify the attempt as mechanically disrupted, release any non-terminal bead claim, remove runtime liveness state, and leave the unfinished bead re-claimable. The current code has partial context-cancellation classification but needs explicit end-to-end coverage for the CLI work/try paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 marks non-success reports as disrupted when ctx is cancelled, but the surrounding cleanup/unclaim behavior is only incidentally covered by generic failure branches.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:812-846 starts and stops claim heartbeat around an attempt; interrupted attempts must stop liveness and release or clear claims deterministically.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:797-807 creates a dispatch context and only the bead-extra cancel path forces `operator_cancel`; plain context cancellation should remain `signal`/mechanical disruption, not `operator_cancel`.
- cli/cmd/try.go:333-342 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1921-1940 propagate `cmd.Context()` into worker.Run but need regression tests that cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and reports the signal path.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:289-314 defines the claim release and no-cooldown requirements for worker shutdown/interruption.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make interrupted attempts produce a stable disrupted/interrupted report reason (for example `context_canceled` / `signal`) without counting as model no-progress.
- Ensure the loop unclaims interrupted non-terminal beads and removes any external claim heartbeat/liveness record.
- Ensure interrupted attempts do not set `execute-loop-retry-after` unless a separate retryable condition was already recorded before cancellation.
- Ensure `ddx try <id>` reports a clear interrupted outcome and exits non-zero after cleanup.
- Add regression tests around worker.Run plus command-level try/work cancellation paths using controllable fake executors/runners.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not preserve worktrees as resumable by default. Cleanup/retry-from-scratch is the reliable default.
- Do not alter operator cancel-marker semantics (`cancel-requested` / `operator_cancel`).
- Do not change successful landing/review behavior when cancellation arrives after a terminal mutation.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead verifies cancelling the work command context during an in-flight attempt releases the claim and leaves the bead open/re-claimable.
2. TestWorkInterrupt_DoesNotSetNoProgressCooldown verifies interrupted work does not set `execute-loop-retry-after` or increment no-progress suppression.
3. TestWorkInterrupt_RemovesClaimLiveness verifies external claim heartbeat/liveness is removed or stale-safe after cleanup.
4. TestTryInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsTarget verifies `ddx try <id>` cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and exits non-zero with an interrupted/signal-class outcome.
5. TestTryInterrupt_DoesNotReportOperatorCancel verifies plain context cancellation is not classified as `operator_cancel` unless the bead cancel marker was present.
6. TestInterruptedAfterTerminalMutation_DoesNotUndoClose verifies cancellation after a successful terminal mutation does not reopen or unclaim a closed bead.
7. `rg -n "context.Canceled|context_canceled|signal|operator_cancel|execute-loop-retry-after" cli/internal/agent cli/cmd` shows cancellation paths are explicit and comments distinguish signal interruption from operator cancel.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestWorkInterrupt|TestTryInterrupt|TestInterrupted|TestCancel|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
